internal_anchors.details: test.js Section 3 (Internal Links) passes 3,476/0 — all href="#..." targets and showTab() calls verified by the suite. Spot-check: every WIN id in wins.json (71 entries) has a matching id="winNNN" anchor in docs/index.html (including 057b, 058b).

tab_structure.details: 12 tab-content divs with unique IDs (overview, evaluate, model, selftest, wins, pages, killshots, timestamp, predictions, falsify, ai, refs). sectionNav chain is fully continuous: overview prev=null, refs next=null, every intermediate tab's prev/next matches the linear order. Heading hierarchy clean — no h2 before h1 in any tab.

external_links.details: Of 154 unique external URLs, 85 are non-citation data-source links (citation/DOI/publisher domains skipped per spec). One real 404 (john09289.github.io/predictions/raw-text/03-predictions.txt) and two transient JPL timeouts. NOAA/USGS/ESA/INTERMAGNET/NASA/sciencedirect 403s (HEAD blocked) all resolved on retry with browser UA — not broken.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json = 71 entries. Computed verdict tally {Refuted by Data:9, Self-Contradicted:14, Misleading:22, Std Model Explains:18, Not Demonstrated:4, Unfalsifiable:4} matches the verdict counts rendered in the HTML. uncounted-failures.json has 10 entries; HTML prose says "10 acknowledged failure(s)." Headline "69 Wins Reviewed" reflects the 69 unique base WIN numbers in the dome registry (the 2 extra wins.json entries are paired sub-entries 057b/058b).

win_detail_consistency.details: Every wins.json id (001-069 plus 057b, 058b) has a matching id="winNNN" anchor in docs/index.html. Verdict text appears in each WIN's content block for all 71 entries (verified by sliding-window search between consecutive winNNN anchors).

win_number_alignment.details: Dome wins.html (V51.1, fetched 2026-04-27) lists WIN-001 through WIN-069 confirmed (with WIN-025 marked REMOVED). Our wins.json covers all 69 base numbers plus 057b/058b. The dome-side WIN-062 collision (two visible blocks: 'Tesla Longitudinal Wave Speed = 1.574c' id=win-062 and 'Tesla Longitudinal Constant (1.574c)' id=win-062-constant) is already documented in our review (Section 6.2 / WIN-062 detail tab) and acknowledged by the dome author's 2026-04-17 registry_integrity_notes block — no re-flag.

progressive_disclosure.details: All wins.json entries have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict (0/71 missing). All 13 entries flagged is_genuinely_prospective in predictions.json have a tldr field. Per-tab details/summary/tldr coverage: evaluate 5/5, model 12/13, selftest 18/18, wins 143/8 h2 (per-WIN details), pages 13/14, killshots 8/10, timestamp 6/7, predictions 92/94, falsify 12/12, ai 32/32, refs 4/4. Small h2-vs-details gaps in model/pages/killshots/timestamp/predictions are tab-level section titles or scorecard headings exempted by spec. Overview tab uses scorecard layout (no details wrapper required).

hardcoded_theme_colors.details: Zero hardcoded light backgrounds in sections.json (regex /#(fff|ffffff|f8f9fa|fafafa|f5f5f5|eeeeee|e0e0e0)\b|background:\s*white\b/i, 0 matches).

discoverability_infrastructure.details: docs/llms.txt exists, mentions funwithscience.net/dome-model-review, names the model, references the 71/69 WIN counts correctly (71 entries documenting 69 dome WIN numbers). docs/sitemap.xml is valid XML with two <loc> entries (main page + llms.txt). docs/robots.txt has 'Allow: /' and Sitemap directive pointing to the sitemap URL. <head> contains meta description, og:title, og:description, and an application/ld+json block with ClaimReview. uncounted-failures.json has 10 valid entries (no missing required fields, no duplicate IDs); rendered count '10 acknowledged failure' matches.

expansion_tracker_continuity.details: tracker has 265 items, max_id=267, next_id=268 (next_id > max_id, OK — no collision risk). 2 ID gaps (52, 259) — these are pre-existing intentional skips per prior tracker hygiene, no new gap. 0 orphan files in expansions/, 0 phantom tracker entries (all complete entries with output_file point to existing files), 0 stale-pending items >48h old. 0 coupled-category items missing from priority-queue (section-new + category-proposal-writeup all integrated).

workspace_only_files.details: Two workspace-only files detected vs git: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (0-byte, looks like a stray editor/build temp — should be deleted, not committed) and monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak (21KB backup of EXP-202 from 2026-04-22, 'DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW'). Neither is a completed review or expansion at risk of silent loss; both are detritus. Recommend deletion (or, for the .bak, fold into EXP-202 if the draft contains useful content not yet integrated).

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: Last 200 skip-log lines, 24h window: 0 direction-violation skips, 147 mtime-guard skips (informational — git version newer for files like applied-patches/*.json, daily-report-*.json, category-proposals/CAT-005.json). No repeated direction violations.

Build is fully reproducible from data/wins.json + data/sections.json + data/predictions.json + data/uncounted-failures.json. Test suite passes 3,476/0.

summary: Site is structurally sound. Build is fully reproducible (0 diff lines after node build.js html). Test suite passes 3,476/0. Internal anchors, tab structure, sectionNav chain, heading hierarchy, data-prose verdict tallies, WIN-detail consistency, dome WIN-number alignment, progressive disclosure structure, theme colors, discoverability infrastructure (llms.txt/sitemap.xml/robots.txt/meta tags/ClaimReview JSON-LD), expansion-tracker continuity (next_id=268 > max_id=267, 0 orphans, 0 phantoms, 0 stale pending), priority-queue coupling, workspace-sync skip log, curmudgeon digest coverage (0 parse errors, 7 unprocessed within normal lag), and CLAUDE.md mechanical facts all check clean. Two moderate issues: (1) external link to john09289.github.io/predictions/raw-text/03-predictions.txt returns 404 — dome author renumbered or removed that mirror file; (2) two stray workspace-only files (a 0-byte .build.js in curmudgeon/reviews/ and an EXP-202.json.bak draft backup). Neither blocks publication; both are sweep-cleanup. Overall status: warn (no critical/major findings).
